title: rubus plicatus subsp. bertramii
intro: Rubus plicatus subsp. bertramii is a subspecies of the bramble plant, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and native to the island of Madeira in the Atlantic Ocean. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. Rubus plicatus subsp. bertramii can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
